mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If falling UNRATE is followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window, or if the rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above a 1.0% threshold after a prolonged period below 0.5% signals the beginning of a monetary tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0% after being below 0.5% for at least 3 months
failure_condition: If positive S&P 500 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% under the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Joint consecutive monthly increases in both housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand strength, which boosts aggregate corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Consecutive monthly increases in both HOUST and RSAFS for at least 2 months
failure_condition: If the joint signal yields negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered windows, or if the mean return is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (UNRATE below 5.0%), a rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) intensifies wage inflation pressures, which compresses profit margins and signals overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases in the tight labor market regime lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the negative predictive accuracy fails to exceed 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic weakness, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO y/y negative leads to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 30% of triggered windows, or if the rank IC is below -0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Combined high unemployment (UNRATE > 6%) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%) signal a stagflationary environment where both weak demand and inflation erode corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined signal does not predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with greater than 60% accuracy, or if the mean return is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An ongoing increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If rising RSAFS leads to negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, or if the rank IC is consistently non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year exceeding 3% triggers inflation fears, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy which depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL y/y > 3% leads to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
